revision.text: #redirect[[en:Hikobe_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]
堺市西区草部(くさべ)にある神社。浦島太郎の末裔との伝承もある。ご本殿及び石灯籠は国の重要文化財として登録されている。 日部神社では１０月に五穀豊穣を祈願し地車（だんじり）祭がとり行われている。

revision.comment: {{db|定義未満}}

revision.text:
'''日部神社'''（くさべじんじゃ）とは堺市西区草部(くさべ)にある神社。
浦島太郎の末裔を祭っているとの伝承もある。
ご本殿及び石灯籠は国の重要文化財として登録されている。石燈籠には製作日の記録として、「正平24（1369年）」と彫られている。神社自体もその頃完成したと考えられている。
==祭り== *7月13日 夏祭。 *10月5日 五穀豊穣を祈願し地車（だんじり）祭がとり行われている。
[[Category:大阪府の神社|くさへしんしや]]

'''日部神社'''（くさべじんじゃ）は、[[堺市]]西区草部（くさべ）にある[[神社]]。
[[浦島太郎]]の末裔を祭っているとの伝承もある。
ご本殿及び石灯籠は国の重要文化財として登録されている。石燈籠には製作日の記録として、「正平24（1369年）」と彫られている。神社自体もその頃完成したと考えられている。
==祭り== *7月13日 夏祭。 *10月5日 五穀豊穣を祈願し地車（だんじり）祭がとり行われている。
[[Category:大阪府の神社|くさへしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 体裁をととのへる
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=日部神社| 所在地=[[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部262| 祭神=彦坐王、神武天皇、
道臣命| 社格=式内小社| 創建=| 本殿=切妻造り| 例祭=[[10月5日]]| 画像=}} '''日部神社'''（くさべじんじゃ）は、[[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部（くさべ）にある[[神社]]。[[浦島太郎]]の末裔を祭っているとの伝承もある。 ご本殿及び石灯籠は国の[[重要文化財]]として登録されている。[[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）に重文に登録された本殿は[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]のもので、その蟇股（かえるまた）には、さまざまな動物の彫刻が施されてある。石燈籠には製作日の記録として、「[[正平]]二十四年卯月八日」と彫られている。神社自体もその頃完成したと考えられている。 == 祭事・年中行事 == *[[7月13日]]　夏祭。 *[[10月5日]]　五穀豊穣を祈願し地車（だんじり）祭がとり行われている。 [[Category:大阪府の神社|くさへしんしや]] [[Category:式内小社|くさへしんしゃ]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=日部神社| 所在地=[[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部262| 祭神=彦坐王、神武天皇、
道臣命| 社格=式内小社| 創建=| 本殿=切妻造り| 例祭=[[10月5日]]| 画像=}} '''日部神社'''（くさべじんじゃ）は、[[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部（くさべ）にある[[神社]]。[[浦島太郎]]の末裔を祭っているとの伝承もある。 ご本殿及び石灯籠は国の[[重要文化財]]として登録されている。[[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）に重文に登録された本殿は[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]のもので、その蟇股（かえるまた）には、さまざまな動物の彫刻が施されてある。石燈籠には製作日の記録として、「[[正平]]二十四年卯月八日」と彫られている。神社自体もその頃完成したと考えられている。 == 祭事・年中行事 == *[[7月13日]]　夏祭。 *[[10月5日]]　五穀豊穣を祈願し[[地車]]（だんじり）祭がとり行われている。 [[Category:大阪府の神社|くさへしんしや]] [[Category:式内小社|くさへしんしゃ]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=日部神社| 所在地=[[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部262| 祭神=[[彦坐王]]、[[神武天皇]]、
[[丹波道主王|道臣命]]| 社格=式内小社| 創建=| 本殿=切妻造り| 例祭=[[10月5日]]| 画像=}} '''日部神社'''（くさべじんじゃ）は、[[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部（くさべ）にある[[神社]]。[[浦島太郎]]の末裔を祭っているとの伝承もある。 ご本殿及び石灯籠は国の[[重要文化財]]として登録されている。[[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）に重文に登録された本殿は[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]のもので、その蟇股（かえるまた）には、さまざまな動物の彫刻が施されてある。石燈籠には製作日の記録として、「[[正平]]二十四年卯月八日」と彫られている。神社自体もその頃完成したと考えられている。 == 祭事・年中行事 == *[[7月13日]]　夏祭。 *[[10月5日]]　五穀豊穣を祈願し[[地車]]（だんじり）祭がとり行われている。 [[Category:大阪府の神社|くさへしんしや]] [[Category:式内小社|くさへしんしゃ]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 追記
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=日部神社| 所在地=大阪府堺市西区草部262| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|15|N|135|27|56|E|scale:10000}}| 祭神=彦坐王
神武天皇
道臣命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 創建=| 本殿=[[入母屋造]]| 例祭=[[10月5日]]| 画像=}} '''日部神社'''（くさべじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部（くさべ）にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[彦坐王]]・[[神武天皇]]・[[丹波道主王|道臣命]]を主祭神とする。彦坐命は[[日下部氏|日下部首氏]]の祖神である。日下部首氏はこの一帯を拠点としていた豪族で、一族には[[浦島太郎]]もいたという。 当社は明治時代に鶴田村字輪之内にあった日部神社、同字寺山の八坂神社、原田の熊野神社、上村の菅原神社を[[神社合祀|合祀]]したもので、社名と主祭神は式内社である旧日部神社のものを採用している。他に旧八坂神社の[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、旧熊野神社の[[イザナミ|伊弉冉尊]]、旧菅原神社の[[菅原道真]]を合せ祀る。 ==歴史== 日部神社の創建の由緒は不詳であるが、神武天皇が[[神武東征|東征]]の際に上陸し、長髄彦と最初の戦いをした「日下の蓼津」はこの一帯であると『[[古事記伝]]』に記されている。 明治44年に八坂神社・熊野神社・菅原神社が日部神社に合祀され、旧八坂神社の社地に遷座した。 ==境内== 社地および本殿・神門は旧八坂神社のものである。本殿前の石燈籠に製作日の記録として「[[正平]]二十四年卯月八日」と彫られていることから本殿は[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]から[[室町時代]]にかけての建造とみられ、本殿・石灯籠は[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、[[重要文化財]]に指定された。本殿の[[蟇股]]には、さまざまな彫刻が施されてある。拝殿は旧菅原神社のものを移築したものである。 日部神社の旧社地は現在地の南300mの所で、道臣命が埋葬されていると伝えられる御山古墳の側にある。遷座後の旧社地は大正初年に民間に買却され、現在は住宅地となっている。 == 祭事・年中行事 == *[[7月13日]] 夏祭 *[[10月5日]] 五穀豊穣を祈願し[[地車]]（だんじり）祭がとり行われている。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/izumi/bun/iz050123-01.html 日部神社]（延喜式神社の調査） {{DEFAULTSORT:くさへしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]]

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=日部神社| 所在地=大阪府堺市西区草部262| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|15|N|135|27|56|E|scale:10000}}| 祭神=彦坐王
神武天皇
道臣命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 創建=| 本殿=[[入母屋造]]| 例祭=[[10月5日]]| 画像=}} '''日部神社'''（くさべじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部（くさべ）にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[彦坐王]]・[[神武天皇]]・[[丹波道主王|道臣命]]を主祭神とする。彦坐命は[[日下部氏|日下部首氏]]の祖神である。日下部首氏はこの一帯を拠点としていた豪族で、一族には[[浦島太郎]]もいたという。 当社は明治時代に鶴田村字輪之内にあった日部神社、同字寺山の八坂神社、原田の熊野神社、上村の菅原神社を[[神社合祀|合祀]]したもので、社名と主祭神は式内社である旧日部神社のものを採用している。他に旧八坂神社の[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、旧熊野神社の[[イザナミ|伊弉冉尊]]、旧菅原神社の[[菅原道真]]を合せ祀る。 ==歴史== 日部神社の創建の由緒は不詳であるが、神武天皇が[[神武東征|東征]]の際に上陸し、長髄彦と最初の戦いをした「日下の蓼津」はこの一帯であると『[[古事記伝]]』に記されている。 明治44年に八坂神社・熊野神社・菅原神社が日部神社に合祀され、旧八坂神社の社地に遷座した。 ==境内== 社地および本殿・神門は旧八坂神社のものである。本殿前の石燈籠に製作日の記録として「[[正平 (日本)|正平]]二十四年卯月八日」と彫られていることから本殿は[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]から[[室町時代]]にかけての建造とみられ、本殿・石灯籠は[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、[[重要文化財]]に指定された。本殿の[[蟇股]]には、さまざまな彫刻が施されてある。拝殿は旧菅原神社のものを移築したものである。 日部神社の旧社地は現在地の南300mの所で、道臣命が埋葬されていると伝えられる御山古墳の側にある。遷座後の旧社地は大正初年に民間に買却され、現在は住宅地となっている。 == 祭事・年中行事 == *[[7月13日]] 夏祭 *[[10月5日]] 五穀豊穣を祈願し[[地車]]（だんじり）祭がとり行われている。 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kyoiku/_syougai/_kyouiku/bunkazai/kusabe.html 日部神社本殿]（堺市文化財課） *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kyoiku/_syougai/_kyouiku/bunkazai/isidourou.html 石燈籠]（堺市文化財課） * [http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/izumi/bun/iz050123-01.html 日部神社]（延喜式神社の調査） {{DEFAULTSORT:くさへしんしや}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:西区 (堺市)]]

{{神社| 名称=日部神社| 所在地=大阪府堺市西区草部262| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|15|N|135|27|56|E|scale:10000}}| 祭神=彦坐王
神武天皇
道臣命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 創建=| 本殿=[[入母屋造]]| 例祭=[[10月5日]]| 画像=}} '''日部神社'''（くさべじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部（くさべ）にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[彦坐王]]・[[神武天皇]]・[[丹波道主王|道臣命]]を主祭神とする。彦坐命は[[日下部氏|日下部首氏]]の祖神である。日下部首氏はこの一帯を拠点としていた豪族で、一族には[[浦島太郎]]もいたという。 当社は明治時代に[[鶴田村 (大阪府)|鶴田村]][[大字]]草部[[字]]輪之内にあった日部神社、同字寺山の八坂神社、大字原田の熊野神社、大字上の菅原神社を[[神社合祀|合祀]]したもので、社名と主祭神は式内社である旧日部神社のものを採用している。他に旧八坂神社の[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、旧熊野神社の[[イザナミ|伊弉冉尊]]、旧菅原神社の[[菅原道真]]を合せ祀る。 ==歴史== 日部神社の創建の由緒は不詳であるが、神武天皇が[[神武東征|東征]]の際に上陸し、長髄彦と最初の戦いをした「日下の蓼津」はこの一帯であると『[[古事記伝]]』に記されている。 明治44年に八坂神社・熊野神社・菅原神社が日部神社に合祀され、旧八坂神社の社地に遷座した。 ==境内== 社地および本殿・神門は旧八坂神社のものである。本殿前の石燈籠に製作日の記録として「[[正平 (日本)|正平]]二十四年卯月八日」と彫られていることから本殿は[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]から[[室町時代]]にかけての建造とみられ、本殿・石灯籠は[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、[[重要文化財]]に指定された。本殿の[[蟇股]]には、さまざまな彫刻が施されてある。拝殿は旧菅原神社のものを移築したものである。 日部神社の旧社地は現在地の南300mの所で、道臣命が埋葬されていると伝えられる御山古墳の側にある。遷座後の旧社地は大正初年に民間に買却され、現在は住宅地となっている。 == 祭事・年中行事 == *[[7月13日]] 夏祭 *[[10月5日]] 五穀豊穣を祈願し[[地車]]（だんじり）祭がとり行われている。 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kyoiku/_syougai/_kyouiku/bunkazai/kusabe.html 日部神社本殿]（堺市文化財課） *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kyoiku/_syougai/_kyouiku/bunkazai/isidourou.html 石燈籠]（堺市文化財課） {{DEFAULTSORT:くさへしんしや}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:西区 (堺市)]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]]

{{神社| 名称=日部神社| 画像={{画像募集中|cat=大阪府}}| 所在地=大阪府堺市西区草部262| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|15|N|135|27|56|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=日部神社}}| 祭神=彦坐王

道臣命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 創建=| 本殿=[[入母屋造]]| 例祭=[[10月5日]]|}} '''日部神社'''（くさべじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部（くさべ）にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[彦坐王]]・[[神武天皇]]・[[丹波道主王|道臣命]]を主祭神とする。彦坐命は[[日下部氏|日下部首氏]]の祖神である。日下部首氏はこの一帯を拠点としていた豪族で、一族には[[浦島太郎]]もいたという。 当社は明治時代に[[鶴田村 (大阪府)|鶴田村]][[大字]]草部[[字]]輪之内にあった日部神社、同字寺山の八坂神社、大字原田の熊野神社、大字上の菅原神社を[[神社合祀|合祀]]したもので、社名と主祭神は式内社である旧日部神社のものを採用している。他に旧八坂神社の[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、旧熊野神社の[[イザナミ|伊弉冉尊]]、旧菅原神社の[[菅原道真]]を合せ祀る。 ==歴史== 日部神社の創建の由緒は不詳であるが、神武天皇が[[神武東征|東征]]の際に上陸し、長髄彦と最初の戦いをした「日下の蓼津」はこの一帯であると『[[古事記伝]]』に記されている。 明治44年に八坂神社・熊野神社・菅原神社が日部神社に合祀され、旧八坂神社の社地に遷座した。 ==境内== 社地および本殿・神門は旧八坂神社のものである。本殿前の石燈籠に製作日の記録として「[[正平 (日本)|正平]]二十四年卯月八日」と彫られていることから本殿は[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]から[[室町時代]]にかけての建造とみられ、本殿・石灯籠は[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、[[重要文化財]]に指定された。本殿の[[蟇股]]には、さまざまな彫刻が施されてある。拝殿は旧菅原神社のものを移築したものである。 日部神社の旧社地は現在地の南300mの所で、道臣命が埋葬されていると伝えられる御山古墳の側にある。遷座後の旧社地は大正初年に民間に買却され、現在は住宅地となっている。 == 祭事・年中行事 == *[[7月13日]] 夏祭 *[[10月5日]] 五穀豊穣を祈願し[[地車]]（だんじり）祭がとり行われている。 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kyoiku/_syougai/_kyouiku/bunkazai/kusabe.html 日部神社本殿]（堺市文化財課） *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kyoiku/_syougai/_kyouiku/bunkazai/isidourou.html 石燈籠]（堺市文化財課） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くさへしんしや}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:日下部氏|社くさへしんしや]]

{{神社| 名称=日部神社| 画像=[[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]]| 所在地=大阪府堺市西区草部262| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=日部神社}}| 祭神=彦坐王
神武天皇
道臣命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 創建=| 本殿=[[入母屋造]]| 例祭=[[10月5日]]|}} '''日部神社'''（くさべじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部（くさべ）にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[彦坐王]]・[[神武天皇]]・[[丹波道主王|道臣命]]を主祭神とする。彦坐命は[[日下部氏|日下部首氏]]の祖神である。日下部首氏はこの一帯を拠点としていた豪族で、一族には[[浦島太郎]]もいたという。 当社は明治時代に[[鶴田村 (大阪府)|鶴田村]][[大字]]草部[[字]]輪之内にあった日部神社、同字寺山の八坂神社、大字原田の熊野神社、大字上の菅原神社を[[神社合祀|合祀]]したもので、社名と主祭神は式内社である旧日部神社のものを採用している。他に旧八坂神社の[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、旧熊野神社の[[イザナミ|伊弉冉尊]]、旧菅原神社の[[菅原道真]]を合せ祀る。 ==歴史== 日部神社の創建の由緒は不詳であるが、'''神武天皇'''が[[神武東征|東征]]の際に上陸し、長髄彦と最初の戦いをした「日下の蓼津」はこの一帯であると『[[古事記伝]]』に記されている。 明治44年に八坂神社・熊野神社・菅原神社が日部神社に合祀され、旧八坂神社の社地に遷座した。 ==境内== 社地および本殿・神門は旧八坂神社のものである。本殿前の石燈籠に製作日の記録として「[[正平 (日本)|正平]]二十四年卯月八日」と彫られていることから本殿は[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]から[[室町時代]]にかけての建造とみられ、本殿・石灯籠は[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、[[重要文化財]]に指定された。本殿の[[蟇股]]には、さまざまな彫刻が施されてある。拝殿は旧菅原神社のものを移築したものである。 日部神社の旧社地は現在地の南300mの所で、道臣命が埋葬されていると伝えられる御山古墳の側にある。遷座後の旧社地は大正初年に民間に買却され、現在は住宅地となっている。 == 祭事・年中行事 == *[[7月13日]] 夏祭 *[[10月5日]] 五穀豊穣を祈願し[[地車]]（だんじり）祭がとり行われている。 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kyoiku/_syougai/_kyouiku/bunkazai/kusabe.html 日部神社本殿]（堺市文化財課） *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kyoiku/_syougai/_kyouiku/bunkazai/isidourou.html 石燈籠]（堺市文化財課） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くさへしんしや}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:日下部氏|社くさへしんしや]] [[Category:堺市西区の建築物]]

=== 堺市指定文化財 === *神門 - 江戸時代の作[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html 日部神社神門 堺市]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 日部神社 堺観光ガイド] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くさへしんしや}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:日下部氏|社くさへしんしや]] [[Category:堺市西区の建築物]]

背面[[入母屋造]]| 例祭=[[10月5日]]|}} '''日部神社'''（くさべじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部にある神社。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[彦坐王]]・[[神武天皇]]・[[道臣命]]を主祭神とする。彦坐命は[[日下部氏|日下部首氏]]の祖神である。日下部首氏はこの一帯を拠点としていた豪族で、一族には[[浦島太郎]]もいたという。 当社は明治時代に[[鶴田村 (大阪府)|鶴田村]][[大字]]草部[[字]]輪之内にあった日部神社、同字寺山の八坂神社、大字原田の熊野神社、大字上の菅原神社を[[神社合祀|合祀]]したもので、社名と主祭神は式内社である旧日部神社のものを採用している。他に旧八坂神社の[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、旧熊野神社の[[イザナミ|伊弉冉尊]]、旧菅原神社の[[菅原道真]]を合せ祀る。 ==歴史== 日部神社の創建の由緒は不詳であるが、[[神武天皇]]が[[神武東征|東征]]の際に上陸し、[[長髄彦]]と最初の戦いをした「日下の蓼津」はこの一帯であると『[[古事記伝]]』に記されている。 明治44年に八坂神社・熊野神社・菅原神社が日部神社に合祀され、旧八坂神社の社地に遷座した。 ==境内== 社地および本殿・神門は旧八坂神社のものである。本殿前の石燈籠に製作日の記録として「[[正平 (日本)|正平]]二十四年卯月八日」と彫られていることから本殿は[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]から[[室町時代]]にかけての建造とみられ、本殿・石燈籠は[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、[[重要文化財]]に指定された。『[[和泉名所図会]]』（[[寛政]]8年（1796年））第2巻[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2563478/36 『和泉名所図会』第2巻]によれば、これは南北朝時代を代表する名将[[楠木正儀]]の寄進によるものと言われる（詳細は[[楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など]]）。本殿の[[蟇股]]には、さまざまな彫刻が施されてある。拝殿は旧菅原神社のものを移築したものである。 日部神社の旧社地は現在地の南300mの所で、道臣命が埋葬されていると伝えられる御山古墳の側にある。遷座後の旧社地は大正初年に民間に買却され、現在は住宅地となっている。 == 祭事・年中行事 == *夏祭（7月13日） *地車（だんじり）祭（10月5日） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *本殿 - 南北朝時代の作[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html 日部神社本殿 堺市]。 *石燈籠 - 南北朝時代 正平24年(1369年)の作[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html 石燈籠 堺市]。
=== 堺市指定文化財 === *神門 - 江戸時代の作[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html 日部神社神門 堺市]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 日部神社 堺観光ガイド] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くさへしんしや}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:日下部氏|社くさへしんしや]] [[Category:堺市西区の建築物]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]]

[[神武天皇]]
[[道臣命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）、旧[[村社]] | 創建= | 本殿=正面[[切妻造]]
背面[[入母屋造]] | 例祭=[[10月5日]] }} '''日部神社'''（くさべじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]草部にある神社。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== [[彦坐王]]・[[神武天皇]]・[[道臣命]]を主祭神とする。彦坐命は[[日下部氏|日下部首氏]]の祖神である。日下部首氏はこの一帯を拠点としていた豪族で、一族には[[浦島太郎]]もいたという。 当社は明治時代に[[鶴田村 (大阪府)|鶴田村]][[大字]]草部[[字]]輪之内にあった日部神社、同字寺山の八坂神社、大字原田の熊野神社、大字上の菅原神社を[[神社合祀|合祀]]したもので、社名と主祭神は式内社である旧日部神社のものを採用している。他に旧八坂神社の[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、旧熊野神社の[[イザナミ|伊弉冉尊]]、旧菅原神社の[[菅原道真]]を合せ祀る。 ==歴史== 日部神社の創建の由緒は不詳であるが、[[神武天皇]]が[[神武東征|東征]]の際に上陸し、[[長髄彦]]と最初の戦いをした「日下の蓼津」はこの一帯であると『[[古事記伝]]』に記されている。 明治44年に八坂神社・熊野神社・菅原神社が日部神社に合祀され、旧八坂神社の社地に遷座した。 ==境内== 社地および本殿・神門は旧八坂神社のものである。本殿前の石燈籠に製作日の記録として「[[正平 (日本)|正平]]二十四年卯月八日」と彫られていることから本殿は[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]から[[室町時代]]にかけての建造とみられ、本殿・石燈籠は[[大正]]6年（[[1917年]]）、[[重要文化財]]に指定された。『[[和泉名所図会]]』（[[寛政]]8年（1796年））第2巻[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2563478/36 『和泉名所図会』第2巻]によれば、これは南北朝時代を代表する名将[[楠木正儀]]の寄進によるものと言われる（詳細は[[楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など]]）。本殿の[[蟇股]]には、さまざまな彫刻が施されてある。拝殿は旧菅原神社のものを移築したものである。 日部神社の旧社地は現在地の南300mの所で、道臣命が埋葬されていると伝えられる御山古墳の側にある。遷座後の旧社地は大正初年に民間に買却され、現在は住宅地となっている。 == 祭事・年中行事 == *夏祭（7月13日） *地車（だんじり）祭（10月5日） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *本殿 - 南北朝時代の作[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html 日部神社本殿 堺市]。 *石燈籠 - 南北朝時代 正平24年(1369年)の作[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html 石燈籠 堺市]。 === 堺市指定文化財 === *神門 - 江戸時代の作[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html 日部神社神門 堺市]。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 日部神社 堺観光ガイド] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くさへしんしや}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:堺市の重要文化財]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:日下部氏|社くさへしんしや]] [[Category:堺市西区の建築物]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Shrines in Sakai]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Mura-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Sakai City]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:Nishiku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Sakai]]

The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shrines in Sakai]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Former Mura-sha]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Cultural Properties of Sakai City]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Kusakabe clan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Nishiku, Sakai]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:History of Sakai]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Original pages]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[ja:日部神社]]

However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Hikobe construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Hikobe After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Hikobe Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Hikobe Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion
