Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Hikobe Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Hikobe Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Hikobe Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Hikobe Shrine]][[Category:Hikobe Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Hikobe Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Hikobe Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
Rear: {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王}}, {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary {{ill|Urashima Tarō|ja|浦島太郎}}. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)|ja|八坂神社}}, {{ill|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)|ja|熊野神社}}, and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the {{ill|Kojiki-den|ja|古事記伝}}, the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦}} during his {{ill|Eastern Expedition|ja|神武東征}}, landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Hikobe Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori|ja|楠木正儀}} (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 *
