{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}}
Rear: [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiku, Sakai City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]]. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)]], [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)]], and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the [[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]], the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagazumi-hiko|1=Nagasunehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4922278}} during his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]], landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general [[:en:Kusunoki Masanori|Kusunoki Masanori]] (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|1=Kusunoki Masanori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541193}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimagata|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} {{translated page|ja|日部神社|version=78605344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] }} [[ja:日部神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Kusakabe Clan]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11510526}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general [[:en:Kusunoki Masanori|Kusunoki Masanori]] (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|1=Kusunoki Masanori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541193}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimagata|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} {{translated page|ja|日部神社|version=78605344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] }} [[ja:日部神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Kusakabe Clan]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11510526}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}}

[[ja:日部神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.520889|N|135.465667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}}}}{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11510526]] Wikidata name: Kusabe-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}}
Rear: [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiku, Sakai City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]]. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)]], [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)]], and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the [[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]], the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagazumi-hiko|1=Nagasunehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4922278}} during his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]], landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general [[:en:Kusunoki Masanori|Kusunoki Masanori]] (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|1=Kusunoki Masanori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541193}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimagata|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} {{translated page|ja|日部神社|version=78605344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] }} [[ja:日部神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Kusakabe Clan]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11510526}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

[[ja:日部神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.520889|N|135.465667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}}}}{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11510526]] Wikidata name: Kusabe-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}}
Rear: [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiku, Sakai City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]]. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)]], [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)]], and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the [[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]], the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagazumi-hiko|1=Nagasunehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4922278}} during his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]], landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general [[:en:Kusunoki Masanori|Kusunoki Masanori]] (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|1=Kusunoki Masanori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541193}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimagata|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} {{translated page|ja|日部神社|version=78605344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] }} [[ja:日部神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Kusakabe Clan]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11510526}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)]], [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)]], and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the [[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]], the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagazumi-hiko|1=Nagasunehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4922278}} during his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]], landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general [[:en:Kusunoki Masanori|Kusunoki Masanori]] (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|1=Kusunoki Masanori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541193}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimagata|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} {{translated page|ja|日部神社|version=78605344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] }} [[ja:日部神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Kusakabe Clan]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11510526}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kusabe Shrine (Q11510526)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ōtori district|ja|大鳥郡|zh|大鳥郡|WD=Q3270651}} || {{ill|Kusabe Shrine|lt=Kusabe Shrine|ja|日部神社|WD=Q11510526}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.520914|135.465701|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180990 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11510526}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]]

[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}}
Rear: [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiku, Sakai City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]]. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)]], [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)]], and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the [[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]], the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagazumi-hiko|1=Nagasunehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4922278}} during his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]], landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general [[:en:Kusunoki Masanori|Kusunoki Masanori]] (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|1=Kusunoki Masanori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541193}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimagata|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} {{translated page|ja|日部神社|version=78605344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] }} [[ja:日部神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Kusakabe Clan]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11510526}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kusabe Shrine (Q11510526)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|日部神社|version=78605344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] }} [[ja:日部神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Kusakabe Clan]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11510526}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kusabe Shrine (Q11510526)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! revision.text:
[[ja:日部神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.520889|N|135.465667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}}}}{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11510526]] Wikidata name: Kusabe-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}}
Rear: [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiku, Sakai City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]]. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)]], [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)]], and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the [[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]], the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagazumi-hiko|1=Nagasunehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4922278}} during his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]], landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general [[:en:Kusunoki Masanori|Kusunoki Masanori]] (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|1=Kusunoki Masanori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541193}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimagata|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|日部神社|version=78605344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] }} [[ja:日部神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Kusakabe Clan]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11510526}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kusabe Shrine (Q11510526)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ōtori district|ja|大鳥郡|zh|大鳥郡|WD=Q3270651}} || {{ill|Kusabe Shrine|lt=Kusabe Shrine|ja|日部神社|WD=Q11510526}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.520914|135.465701|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180990 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11510526}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]

Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1917 (Taishō 6). [[ja:日部神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.520889|N|135.465667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}}}}{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11510526]] Wikidata name: Kusabe-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto Shrine | name = Kusabe Shrine (日部神社) | image = [[File:Kusabe Shrine Sakai City.jpg|250px]] | location = Kusabe 262, Nishiku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|31|15.2|N|135|27|56.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kusabe Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]
{{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}}
Rear: [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiku, Sakai City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]]. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)]], [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)]], and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the [[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]], the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagazumi-hiko|1=Nagasunehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4922278}} during his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]], landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general [[:en:Kusunoki Masanori|Kusunoki Masanori]] (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|1=Kusunoki Masanori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541193}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimagata|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|日部神社|version=78605344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] }} [[ja:日部神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Kusakabe Clan]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kusabe Shrine (Q11510526)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ōtori district|ja|大鳥郡|zh|大鳥郡|WD=Q3270651}} || {{ill|Kusabe Shrine|lt=Kusabe Shrine|ja|日部神社|WD=Q11510526}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.520914|135.465701|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180990 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] {{wikidata link|Q11510526}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]]. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)]], [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)]], and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the [[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]], the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagazumi-hiko|1=Nagasunehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4922278}} during his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]], landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general [[:en:Kusunoki Masanori|Kusunoki Masanori]] (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|1=Kusunoki Masanori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541193}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimagata|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|日部神社|version=78605344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] }} [[ja:日部神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Kusakabe Clan]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kusabe Shrine (Q11510526)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} | classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = | main_building = Front: {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}}
Rear: [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] | annual_festival = October 5 }} '''Kusabe Shrine''' (日部神社, Kusabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Nishiku, Sakai City|ja|堺市西区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiku, Sakai City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozatsuo|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozatsuo|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], and {{ill|Michinomikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}}. Hikozatsuo is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}, a prominent local ruling family in the area, which is also said to include the legendary [[:en:Urashima Tarō|Urashima Tarō]]. The shrine as it stands today was established in the Meiji period after merging with three other local shrines: [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Osaka)]], [[:en:Kumano shrine|Kumano Shrine (Osaka)]], and {{ill|Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine (Osaka)|1=Sugawara Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48532247}}. The name and main deity are derived from the original Kusabe Shrine, while additional deities from the merged shrines include {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. == History == The exact origin of Kusabe Shrine is unclear. However, according to the [[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]], the area surrounding the shrine is believed to be where Emperor Jimmu first engaged in battle with {{ill|Nagazumi-hiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagazumi-hiko|1=Nagasunehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4922278}} during his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Expedition]], landing at the port of Kusaka no Tadezu. In 1911 (Meiji 44), Kusabe Shrine was merged with Yasaka Shrine, Kumano Shrine, and Sugawara Shrine and relocated to the former site of Yasaka Shrine. == Grounds == The current shrine precinct, including the main hall and shrine gate, originally belonged to Yasaka Shrine. A stone lantern near the main hall is inscribed with the date "April 8, Shōhei 24" (1369), indicating that the structure dates back to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]] to the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. Both the main hall and stone lantern were designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1917 (Taishō 6). According to the {{ill|Izumi Meisho Zue|ja|和泉名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1796), the shrine was said to have been rebuilt through donations by the renowned general [[:en:Kusunoki Masanori|Kusunoki Masanori]] (see {{ill|Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|ja|楠木正儀#墓所・史跡など|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki Masanori#Tomb and Historical Sites|1=Kusunoki Masanori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541193}}). The main hall features elaborate carvings on its {{ill|Kimagata|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimagata|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}}. The worship hall was relocated from the former Sugawara Shrine. The original site of Kusabe Shrine, located 300 meters south of the current location, is near the {{ill|Miyama Kofun|ja|御山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where Michinomikoto is said to be buried. After the relocation, the old site was sold to private ownership in the early Taishō era and has since become a residential area. == Festivals and Annual Events == * Summer Festival: July 13 * {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} Festival: October 5 == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall - Nanboku-chō period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabehonden.html Kusabe Shrine Main Hall, Sakai City] * Stone Lantern - Nanboku-chō period, dated to Shōhei 24 (1369).[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/ishidoro.html Stone Lantern, Sakai City] === Sakai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shrine Gate - Edo period construction.[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/kusabeshinmon.html Kusabe Shrine Gate, Sakai City] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/204 Kusabe Shrine - Sakai Tourism Guide] {{Shinto}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|日部神社|version=78605344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] }} [[ja:日部神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Kusakabe Clan]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kusabe Shrine (Q11510526)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ōtori district|ja|大鳥郡|zh|大鳥郡|WD=Q3270651}} || {{ill|Kusabe Shrine|lt=Kusabe Shrine|ja|日部神社|WD=Q11510526}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.520914|135.465701|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180990 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] {{wikidata link|Q11510526}}==Notes==
